mnemonics: King Philip Came Over For Good Spaghetti
keyPoints: Inner products are used to measure distances and angles between vectors.
Inner product spaces are vector spaces with an inner product defined on them.
Length (norm) is the square root of the inner product of a vector with itself.
Orthogonality is when the inner product of two vectors is zero.
Orthogonal sets are sets of vectors where each vector is orthogonal to every other vector.
A basis is a set of orthogonal vectors that span the vector space.
Projections are used to find the component of a vector in the direction of another vector.
The Gram-Schmidt process is a method for creating an orthogonal basis from a given set of vectors.
QR-factorization is a method for decomposing a matrix into a product of an orthogonal matrix and an upper triangular matrix.
Least squares is a method for finding the best fit line or plane through a set of data points.
